title: SFC reprimands and fines licensed corporation for AML control failures
body: The Securities and Futures Commission reprimanded a licensed corporation and imposed a fine after identifying anti-money laundering control deficiencies. The firm failed to perform sufficient customer due diligence and transaction monitoring for high-risk clients.
summary: Title: SFC reprimands and fines licensed corporation for AML control failures Body: The Securities and Futures Commission reprimanded a licensed corporation and imposed a fine after identifying anti-money laundering control deficiencies. The firm failed to perform sufficient customer due diligence and transaction monitoring for high-risk clients.
obligations: Review CDD and EDD procedures for high-risk clients
Check transaction monitoring alerts and escalation evidence

title: Circular to licensed corporations on market misconduct surveillance
body: The regulator reminded licensed corporations to maintain effective trading surveillance, escalation procedures, and evidence retention. Firms should review market conduct controls for suspicious order patterns and disclosure obligations.
summary: Title: Circular to licensed corporations on market misconduct surveillance Body: The regulator reminded licensed corporations to maintain effective trading surveillance, escalation procedures, and evidence retention. Firms should review market conduct controls for suspicious order patterns and disclosure obligations.
obligations: Review trading surveillance scenarios and alert thresholds
Retain evidence for suspicious order pattern escalation

title: Updated licensing guidance for responsible officers
body: The SFC published updated licensing guidance for responsible officers and representatives. The update clarifies competence requirements, sponsor responsibilities, and ongoing notification expectations.
summary: Title: Updated licensing guidance for responsible officers Body: The SFC published updated licensing guidance for responsible officers and representatives. The update clarifies competence requirements, sponsor responsibilities, and ongoing notification expectations.
obligations: Check responsible officer and representative competence records
